<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by ~/.claude/hooks/gen-invoke-commands.py from autonomous-skill-router.config.json вЂ” do not edit by hand; edit the config and re-run the generator. -->

This is the orchestrated **IMPLEMENT + DESIGN + CLEANUP** suite (3-act). Execute the acts IN ORDER, top to bottom, without asking for permission except at marked CHECKPOINTs. Artifacts are files on disk вЂ” a broken chain resumes from the last artifact instead of restarting.

## ACT A вЂ” INTEL (do this yourself, no dispatch)

1. jcodemunch: `plan_turn` (query = the user's request) + `assemble_task_context` on the impacted area.
2. graphify: `graph_stats` + `query_graph` for the impacted area (freshness check; rebuild only if a guard reported STALE).
3. Read `CODEX.md` + the repo root `CLAUDE.md` if present (dox tree: root в†’ target dir).
4. Produce the **BRIEF**: task, surfaces (FE/BE/infra), impacted layers, contracts at risk, relevant paths.

Every agent dispatched below receives this BRIEF вЂ” specialists start warm, never blind.

## ACT B вЂ” DISPATCH (in this order)

### IMPLEMENT в†’ implementation-engineer  вљ пёЏ RUNS ON OPUS (mandatory)

> вљ пёЏ **RUNS ON OPUS (mandatory).** Every step of this **IMPLEMENT** task is done by OPUS, never a smaller model. Spawn all implement subagents with a `[opus]` description prefix **and** `model:"opus"` (opus-guard.py then pins them to OPUS). If the main loop is not already on OPUS, delegate the work to a OPUS subagent instead of doing it inline. Read-only helpers (search/explore/lint/docs) may keep their default model.

**REQUIRES a PLAN artifact (`plan-*.md`).** Use the one produced above if the PLAN act ran. If none exists on disk, do NOT stop and do NOT ask вЂ” auto-run a mini planning act first: dispatch **planning-director** (`[sonnet]` prefix) with the BRIEF to produce it, then continue.

Spawn agent **implementation-engineer** (Agent tool, subagent_type "implementation-engineer", description prefix `[opus] `, model:"opus") with: the BRIEF + its reading list (the agent file already carries it; the same skills appear in the FALLBACK block below).
Wait for artifact `IMPL-REPORT.md`.

### DESIGN в†’ frontend-uiux-designer

Spawn agent **frontend-uiux-designer** (Agent tool, subagent_type "frontend-uiux-designer", description prefix `[opus] `, model:"opus") with: the BRIEF + the prior act's artifact (`IMPL-REPORT.md`) + its reading list (the agent file already carries it; the same skills appear in the FALLBACK block below).
Wait for artifact `DESIGN-REPORT.md`.

### CLEANUP в†’ deadcode-reaper

Spawn agent **deadcode-reaper** (Agent tool, subagent_type "deadcode-reaper", description prefix `[sonnet] `, model:"sonnet") with: the BRIEF + the prior act's artifact (`DESIGN-REPORT.md`) + its reading list (the agent file already carries it; the same skills appear in the FALLBACK block below).
Wait for artifact `REAP-REPORT.md`.

## ACT C вЂ” CLOSE (automatic вЂ” do not skip, do not ask)

This suite mutates code, so the closers run unconditionally. Dispatch each (`[sonnet]` prefix) with the BRIEF + the diff scope, in order:

1. **docs-sync-agent** вЂ” Phase-7 doc sync вЂ” CLAUDE.md tree, server_docs/frontend_docs, CODEX в†’ `DOCS-SYNC-REPORT.md`
2. **qa-verifier** вЂ” end-to-end functional verification of what shipped в†’ `VERIFY-REPORT.md`
3. **security-sentinel** (conditional) вЂ” dispatch ONLY when the diff touches auth/input/API-ish paths (see `orchestration.security_trigger_globs` in the router config) в†’ `SECURITY-REPORT.md`

Each closer writes its report. Then give the user ONE summary: what shipped, evidence, artifact paths.

## FALLBACK вЂ” legacy inline mode

Use ONLY if agent dispatch is unavailable (offline, subagent limits, Agent tool missing). Degrade gracefully: invoke the skills below yourself via the Skill tool, in order, and execute every act sequentially in this context. Never silently drop a category.

**[IMPLEMENT]** вЂ” stack-aware: load cross-cutting ALWAYS; add backend (Go/server) and/or frontend (React/Vite) per repo:

Spawn all implement subagents with a `[opus]` description prefix **and** `model:"opus"` (opus-guard.py then pins them to OPUS). If the main loop is not already on OPUS, delegate the work to a OPUS subagent instead of doing it inline. Read-only helpers (search/explore/lint/docs) may keep their default model.
